title: Gradient from rise and run
problem: A line rises $8$ units for every $4$ units it runs to the right. Find the gradient.

steps.instruction: Substitute the rise and run values.

title: Gradient from two points
problem: Find the gradient of the line passing through $(3, 4)$ and $(7, 12)$.

steps.instruction: Label the coordinates: let $(x_1, y_1) = (3, 4)$ and $(x_2, y_2) = (7, 12)$.
Simplify the numerator and denominator, then divide.

title: Equation of a line from two points
problem: Find the equation of the line passing through $(1, 4)$ and $(3, 10)$.

steps.instruction: Find the gradient using the two points.
Use $y = mx + b$ and substitute one point to find the $y$-intercept. Use $(1, 4)$.
Check by substituting the other point $(3, 10)$.
